gvs.purpose: Detects accountability gaps in the governance structure — governance roles or rules that exist on paper but are not actively enforced or owned.

variables.meaning: Share of governance rules currently marked inactive
Mean compliance (0–1) across only the active rules

gvs.source: ROGF construct — replace with your literature citation on governance void / institutional voids (e.g., Khanna & Palepu-style institutional void theory adapted to platform ecosystems).

aor.purpose: Measures whether the ecosystem is onboarding actors fast enough relative to its declared actor base to sustain network growth.

variables.meaning: Actors currently in Active status
All registered actors regardless of status

aor.source: ROGF construct — replace with your literature citation on ecosystem actor onboarding / platform participation growth.

cri.purpose: Quantifies how concentrated critical dependencies are on a single actor — a proxy for single-point-of-failure exposure.
cri.formulaText: CRI = max_a( Σ dependencyStrength(a) ) / Σ_all( dependencyStrength × 1.5 ) × 100

variables.meaning: Sum of incoming dependency strength, plus half of outgoing dependency strength, for actor a

cri.source: ROGF construct — adapted from Herfindahl-Hirschman concentration index methodology, commonly used in market/network concentration analysis.

pcm.purpose: Assesses whether the platform's technical/orchestration capability and openness are mature enough to support ecosystem-wide coordination.

variables.meaning: Ecosystem-level platform openness setting (0–100)
Mean authority level across actors flagged as platform members

pcm.source: ROGF construct — replace with your literature citation on platform capability / orchestration maturity (e.g., Tiwana-style platform architecture literature).

dsc.purpose: Tracks compliance with data-related governance rules (sovereignty, residency, access control) — critical for cross-border MaaS data flows.

variables.meaning: Governance rules in category "Data" that are currently active
All governance rules in category "Data"

dsc.source: ROGF construct — replace with your literature citation on data sovereignty / GDPR-aligned data governance in industrial ecosystems.

rc.purpose: Captures the social/relational capital embedded in the ecosystem — the average trust actors place in one another and the platform.

variables.meaning: Actor-level trust score (0–100), set at registration or updated over time

rc.source: ROGF construct — replace with your literature citation on relational/social capital in inter-organizational networks (e.g., Nahapiet & Ghoshal social capital theory).

spi.purpose: Detects the 'service paradox' — actors bearing high criticality burden without commensurate realized trust, a known risk in servitized business models.

variables.meaning: Numeric mapping of actor criticality: Low=25, Medium=50, High=75, Critical=100
Actor-level trust score (0–100)

spi.source: ROGF construct — replace with your literature citation on the servitization / service paradox (e.g., Gebauer et al. service paradox literature).

ra.purpose: Estimates the ecosystem's capacity to absorb shocks (actor exit, cyberattack, platform failure) without a governance breakdown.

variables.meaning: Concentration Risk Index (see above)
Share of Critical/High dependencies not in Active status
Compliance level of the Capacity Redundancy Rule (G14), if active

ra.source: ROGF construct — replace with your literature citation on resilience engineering / absorptive capacity (e.g., Hollnagel resilience engineering; Zahra & George absorptive capacity).

ccr.purpose: Measures whether circularity-oriented actors (recyclers, material recovery) are present and meaningfully connected into the material flow.

variables.meaning: Share of all actors that are circularity-oriented (type Recycler or matching capability keywords)
Mean dependency strength of links touching a circular actor

ccr.source: ROGF construct — replace with your literature citation on circular economy closure rates (e.g., Geissdoerfer et al. circular economy literature).

alr.purpose: Proxies how quickly actors absorb and apply governance learning, blending authority and trust as an institutional-learning indicator.

variables.meaning: Actor-level trust score (0–100)
Actor-level authority/decision power score (0–100)
Flat 5-point penalty applied while any actor is still in Onboarding status

alr.source: ROGF construct — replace with your literature citation on organizational learning in inter-firm networks (e.g., Argote organizational learning theory).
